title: System and method for injecting atomized water into an internal combustion engine
abstract: A system for injecting atomized water into an internal combustion engine is provided. The system includes a pressure regulating valve is configured to receive predefined amount of hot air from the engine exhaust via a collecting tube. An atomization unit comprises a water reservoir comprising predefined amount of water; and generator cells operatively coupled to the water reservoir, wherein the plurality of generator cells is configured to receive the predefined amount of hot air from the collecting tube via distributing tubes into the corresponding generator cells; receive a predefined amount of water into the generator cells; and atomize predefined amount of received water by converting the predefined amount of received water into low mass water molecules, high mass water molecules and water droplets. A control valve configured to receive a mixture representative of the low mass water molecules and the hot air, which is recirculated to an air inlet manifold.
claims:
Claims I/WE CLAIM: 1. A system (100) for injecting atomized water into an internal combustion engine, comprising: a pressure regulating valve (110) operatively coupled to an engine exhaust, wherein the pressure regulating valve (110) is configured to receive predefined amount of hot air from the engine exhaust via a collecting tube (108); an atomization unit (116) is operatively coupled to the pressure regulating valve (110), wherein the atomization unit (116) comprises: a water reservoir (118) comprising predefined amount of water; a plurality of generator cells (120) operatively coupled to the water reservoir (118), wherein the plurality of generator cells (120) is configured to: receive the predefined amount of hot air from the collecting tube (108) via a plurality of distributing tubes (114) into the plurality of corresponding generator cells; receive a predefined amount of water into the plurality of generator cells; atomize predefined amount of received water by converting the predefined amount of received water into a plurality of low mass water molecules, a plurality of high mass water molecules and a plurality of water droplets; a control valve (122) is operatively coupled to the water reservoir (118), wherein the control valve (122) is configured to receive a mixture representative of the plurality of low mass water molecules and the hot air, which is recirculated to an air inlet manifold (134). 2. The system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the engine exhaust is operatively coupled to the collecting tube (108). 3. The system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the collecting tube (108) is operatively coupled to the pressure splitter (112) via the pressure regulating valve (110). 4. The system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the splitter (112) is operatively coupled to the plurality of distributing tubes (114). 5. The system (100) as claimed in claim 1, wherein the plurality of generator cells (120) is further configured to convert the plurality of high mass water molecules and the plurality of water droplets into liquid water for reuse by absorbing the plurality of high mass water molecules and the plurality of water droplets. 6. A method (200) for injecting atomized water into an internal combustion engine, comprising: receiving, by a pressure regulating valve (110), a predefined amount of hot air from an engine exhaust via a collecting tube (108); receiving, by a plurality of generator cells (120), the predefined amount of hot air from the collecting tube (108) via a plurality of distributing tubes (114) into the plurality of corresponding generator cells (120); receiving a predefined amount of water into the plurality of generator cells (120); atomizing, by an atomization unit, predefined amount of received water by converting the predefined amount of received water into a plurality of low mass water molecules, a plurality of high mass water molecules and a plurality of water droplets; receiving, by a control valve (122), a mixture representative of the low mass water molecules and the hot air, which is passed to an air inlet manifold (134). 7. The method (200) as claimed in claim 6, receiving, by a plurality of generator cells (120), the predefined amount of hot air from the collecting tube (108) via a plurality of distributing tubes (114) into the plurality of corresponding generator 

cells(120), wherein the plurality of generator cells (120)receives the predefined amount of hot air from the engine exhaust via the pressure splitter (112). 8. The method (200) as claimed in claim 6, wherein receiving, by the control valve (122), the mixture representative of the low mass water molecules and the hot air, is passed to an air inlet manifold (134), due to pressure and temperature of the mixture via the water reservoir (118). 9. The method (200) as claimed in claim 6, further comprising converting, by the plurality of generator cells (120), the plurality of high mass water molecules and the plurality of water droplets into liquid water for reuse by absorbing the plurality of high mass water molecules and the plurality of water droplets.
